guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: Antibiotic — Cellulitis and erysipelas (eTG)
ANZ amputee rehabilitation / residual-limb skin-care guidance
intentional_test_features: Diagnostic overshadowing trap: a new infected residual-limb ulcer is embedded in a story dominated by a phantom-limb pain flare in a known amputee — the risk is dismissing the acute infection as 'just part of being an amputee'.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person clinician who inspected/palpated the stump, took vitals, and prescribed antibiotics; the AI Doctor cannot examine the wound, take vitals without a device, or prescribe remotely, and is scored on recognition + arranging urgent same-day in-person assessment.

Compromised-host calibration: a superficial ulcer with early surrounding cellulitis in an amputated limb is higher-risk than the same finding in an intact limb — the narrow telehealth uplift to same-day in-person (T3) applies because progressive cellulitis/osteomyelitis cannot be excluded remotely.

Bidirectional tiering: this is an ACUTE complication of a stable disability, not stable baseline — tier to true acuity (same-day urgent, T3), while holding a mandatory ED/000 safety-net for systemic sepsis features that are currently absent.
Source category: DCD (Disabilities & Congenital Disorders), source note DCD-001.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
